description: Deck-spec authoring benchmark for the Presentation Designer role. Every task asks for a render-ready 5-slide JSON deck spec for the HTML/CSS deck pipeline: title -> bullets -> image -> quote -> closing title. The judge scores pacing, copy tightness, narration naturalness, slide-type fit, image-cue usefulness, and domain-specific clarity/restraint. Replicated prompts (r1/r2/r3) measure variance on the same Stack Lab and Cashflow Compass briefs.

tasks.title: Stack Lab — The Antigravity CLI, explained (run 1)
tasks.prompt: Author a render-ready deck-spec JSON for the Stack Lab presentation pipeline. TOPIC FACTS: - Tool: The Antigravity CLI. - Core idea: it is a terminal built for agents, not one-command-at-a-time use. - Concrete mechanism: the user gives it a goal, it plans the work, then fans out to subagents that can read files, patch files, and run tests in parallel. - Viewer payoff: work feels less like typing every step and more like directing a coordinated system. AUDIENCE: - Solo builders and operators who already use developer tools. GOAL: - Explain why this tool changes how work feels, not just what command it runs. - Target about a 50-second narrated deck. RETURN ONLY JSON with this exact outer shape: { "theme": {"name":"STACK LAB", "footerL":"stacklab.dev"}, "slides": [ {"type":"title", ...}, {"type":"bullets", ...}, {"type":"image", ...}, {"type":"quote", ...}, {"type":"title", ...} ] } HARD REQUIREMENTS: - Exactly 5 slides, in exactly that slide-type order. - Every slide must include a non-empty `vo` field. - The bullets slide must have exactly 3 bullets and each bullet should stay concise. - The image slide must set `image` to `/tmp/deck-placeholder.png`; use `body` and `caption` to describe the intended still/b-roll cue clearly enough that a human could source or recreate it later. - Do not invent a real-person quote. If you need a quote slide, write a short original line and attribute it to the point of view or concept. - The closing title slide should land the payoff or CTA; it must not simply repeat slide 1. - Spoken VO should sound natural in plain English, 1-3 sentences per slide. - No markdown fences, no notes, no explanation outside the JSON.

criteria.name: structure_pacing
criteria.guidance: Feels like a real ~50-second explainer deck: strong hook, clean middle progression, and a close that lands. Information density is controlled slide to slide; nothing feels bloated or underwritten.

criteria.name: copy_tightness
criteria.guidance: Titles, subtitle, bullets, caption, and quote are concise, specific, and non-generic. Penalize empty hype like 'AI is changing everything' or copy that could fit any tool.

criteria.name: narration_naturalness
criteria.guidance: VO sounds spoken, not like pasted article prose. Sentences transition cleanly between slides, with clear rhythm and no robotic repetition.

criteria.name: slide_type_fit
criteria.guidance: Each slide type is used well for its beat: bullets clarify, image creates a visual moment, quote earns emphasis, closing title provides payoff/CTA instead of recap filler.

criteria.name: image_cue_usefulness
criteria.guidance: The image slide's body/caption describe a concrete, sourceable, on-brand still or b-roll cue that strengthens the explanation. Penalize vague cues like 'futuristic interface' or cues that do not visualize the point.

criteria.name: tool_explanation_quality
criteria.guidance: Accurately explains the Antigravity CLI as a goal-based, agent-first terminal that plans and fans out to subagents for parallel read/patch/test work, and makes the operator payoff legible. Penalize factual drift, generic AI-tool chatter, or missing the 'direct work, not commands' shift.

tasks.title: Cashflow Compass — HSA triple-tax advantage (run 1)
tasks.prompt: Author a render-ready deck-spec JSON for the Cashflow Compass presentation pipeline. TOPIC FACTS: - Subject: the HSA triple-tax advantage. - The three parts to explain are: pre-tax contributions, tax-free growth, and tax-free qualified medical withdrawals. - This is general education for beginners, not personal tax advice. - Avoid current-year contribution limits, tax tables, or any claim that depends on a specific filing situation. AUDIENCE: - US beginners who have heard of an HSA but do not yet understand why people call it uniquely tax-efficient. GOAL: - Make the concept feel clear, calm, and useful without sounding like sales copy or personalized advice. - Target about a 50-second narrated deck. RETURN ONLY JSON with this exact outer shape: { "theme": {"name":"CASHFLOW COMPASS", "footerL":"cashflowcompass.co"}, "slides": [ {"type":"title", ...}, {"type":"bullets", ...}, {"type":"image", ...}, {"type":"quote", ...}, {"type":"title", ...} ] } HARD REQUIREMENTS: - Exactly 5 slides, in exactly that slide-type order. - Every slide must include a non-empty `vo` field. - The bullets slide must have exactly 3 bullets and each bullet should stay concise. - The image slide must set `image` to `/tmp/deck-placeholder.png`; use `body` and `caption` to describe the intended still/b-roll cue clearly enough that a human could source or recreate it later. - Do not invent a real-person quote or a fake statistic. If you need a quote slide, write a short original line and attribute it to the idea or saver perspective. - The closing title slide should land the takeaway; it must not simply repeat slide 1. - Spoken VO should sound calm and plain, 1-3 sentences per slide. - No markdown fences, no notes, no explanation outside the JSON.

criteria.name: structure_pacing
criteria.guidance: Feels like a real ~50-second explainer deck: strong hook, clean middle progression, and a close that lands. Information density is controlled slide to slide; nothing feels bloated or underwritten.

criteria.name: copy_tightness
criteria.guidance: Titles, subtitle, bullets, caption, and quote are concise, specific, and plain-spoken. Penalize jargon, filler, fear tactics, or salesy language.

criteria.name: narration_naturalness
criteria.guidance: VO sounds like calm spoken guidance, not compliance sludge or ad copy. Sentences transition cleanly between slides with no robotic repetition.

criteria.name: slide_type_fit
criteria.guidance: Each slide type is used well for its beat: bullets clarify, image creates a visual moment, quote earns emphasis, closing title provides takeaway instead of recap filler.

criteria.name: image_cue_usefulness
criteria.guidance: The image slide's body/caption describe a concrete, sourceable, non-cliche visual cue that supports the explanation. Penalize generic money imagery, piggy banks, or visuals that do not clarify the point.

criteria.name: accuracy_and_restraint
criteria.guidance: Clearly explains the three HSA tax advantages (pre-tax in, tax-free growth, tax-free qualified medical spending) while staying educational rather than personalized. Penalize invented numbers, current-year limits, fake certainty, or advice that depends on an individual's tax situation.
